Guide

HEX vs RGB vs RGBA

Developers often switch between HEX, RGB, and RGBA when moving colors between design tools, websites, and mobile apps. Each format solves a slightly different problem.

When to Use HEX

  • Design tokens and brand palettes
  • Simple color sharing between teams
  • Frameworks and tools that prefer compact color values

When to Use RGB

  • Channel-level color control
  • CSS and code examples where readability matters
  • Converting from visual picker values

When to Use RGBA

  • Transparent overlays and layered UI
  • Cards, shadows, and tinted backgrounds
  • Any time opacity is part of the design requirement

Try It

Use ColorDevKit to switch instantly between HEX, RGB, RGBA, HSL, HSB, Tailwind, and framework-specific code.
